discount_info: threshold authorities, and contract structuring mechanics for managed enterprise networks

entries.text: Commitments beyond stated SLAs (e.g., 99.999% uptime or <2 hour P1 resolution) require explicit Solution Engineering and Deal Desk pricing reviews.

Bespoke integration development (custom middleware for proprietary customer application alarms) is prohibited under the standard Managed Services MSA and requires a separate Professional Services SOW.
The Pricing Catalog defines standard commercial models, OpEx frameworks, billing triggers, and volume thresholds for VI Business (India).
The document is applicable for designing Commercial Assumptions, OpEx matrices, and TCO evaluations in telecom bounds.
Indicative MRC for Edge Broadband (FTTx) is ₹ 8,000 - ₹ 15,000 per month.
Indicative OTC for Edge Broadband (FTTx) is ₹ 15,000.
Indicative MRC for DIA Enterprise Fiber is ₹ 25,000 - ₹ 75,000 per month.
Indicative OTC for DIA Enterprise Fiber is ₹ 50,000.
Indicative MRC for Active 4G/LTE Backup is ₹ 1,500 - ₹ 3,000 per month.
Indicative OTC for Active 4G/LTE Backup is ₹ 5,000 (including router).
Indicative OTC for Core Hub 1 Gbps PtP is ₹ 1,00,000.
Placeholder INR rates represent standard un-discounted rack rates; bid-specific discounting requires systematic Deal Desk margin validation based on deal TCV.
DIA Enterprise Fiber offers bandwidth capacities ranging from 50 Mbps to 500 Mbps.
DIA Enterprise Fiber pricing ranges from ₹ 25,000 to ₹ 75,000 per month.
DIA Enterprise Fiber charges between ₹ 1,500 and ₹ 3,000 for additional services.
DIA Enterprise Fiber includes a router with an indicative one-time charge of ₹ 5,000.
Core Hub has an indicative one-time charge (OTC) of ₹ 1,00,000.
Connectivity Tier pricing includes an indicative one-time charge (OTC) of ₹ 1,50,000+ (varies by distance).
Hyperscaler Interconnect Subscriptions have bandwidth capacities with indicative monthly recurring charges (MRC) and one-time charges (OTC).
Managed SOC and SIEM Licensing pricing scales based on ingestion volume (Events Per Second or daily log GB) and integrated SD-WAN edge points.
Managed SOC and SIEM Licensing prohibits surprise hourly 'time-and-materials' charges for P1 anomalies.
IoT Enterprise Mobility (EMM) offers volume discounts at scale markers: 100k, 500k, 1M, and 2.5M SIMs.
IoT Enterprise Mobility (EMM) prohibits individual endpoint overage penalties unless total organizational data limits are exceeded.
Pricing is illustrative and subject to regional transmission variants.
Monthly billing for an individual location is securely activated 24 hours after validated ZTP configuration loading and formal User Acceptance Sign-off on stability.
Connectivity Tier pricing is illustrative and subject to regional transmission variants; indicative MRC and OTC values are provided as baseline representations.
Reselling of bare-metal AWS/Azure compute VMs (non-networking) is excluded from VI Business core networking solutions and requires separate systems integration partnerships.
SLA financial credits are capped at 10% to 15% of the offending service's MRC, aligning with liability structures in Standard Terms and Conditions.
The Standard Commercial Policy establishes financial guardrails, hardware leasing mechanisms, discount threshold authorities, and contract structuring mechanics for managed enterprise networks.

The policy applies to Deal Desk, Finance Managers, Sales Leads, and Bid Directors for drafting RFP Commercial Assumptions, generating OpEx TCO evaluations, and detailing minimum contract duration lock-ins.
VI Business (India) must standardize on an end-to-end 'as-a-Service' connectivity model for SD-WAN Edge CPE, cellular failover links, and core SIEM ingestion licenses.
VI retains full capital ownership of all provided hardware throughout the MSA term.
For multi-tiered rollups, billing scales linearly as individual branch site batches go live, with sites lacking active WAN delivery remaining dormant and unbilled.

For long-term MSAs extending beyond 36 months, extreme inflationary fluctuations exceeding standard CPI limits require mandatory commercial indexing protection clauses or re-negotiation gates defined heavily within the master agreement.
The enterprise customer explicitly bears direct financial liability for theft, vandalism, or gross negligence resulting in physical destruction of edge CPE units positioned inside their premises.

Massive IoT fleet distributions utilize aggregate Custom APN data pools to shield customers from erratic billing variations. Individual endpoint overages do not trigger penalty rates unless the total accumulated organizational limit reaches 100% capacity.

Network ports terminating at Azure (ExpressRoute) and AWS (Direct Connect) are priced strictly upon the committed bandwidth pipe. The VI Business MRC does not absorb egress data transfer taxes levied individually by the cloud provider against the customer.
Accumulated monthly SLA financial credits crossing all tracked KPI parameters must never exceed 10% to 15% of that specific, offending link/service’s individual MRC.

Sustained non-payment of uncontested invoices exceeding 60-90 days past the due date structurally empowers VI Business (India) to initiate a staged suspension of non-critical data flows, escalating to hard port isolation, prior to formal contract termination.
Legacy third-party telecom termination fees will not be inherently 'bought out' or subtracted from VI Business (India)'s OpEx proposals without exceptional TCV escalations.

Managed Services are subject to a minimum lock-in period of 36 months. Termination for convenience during this period requires the Customer to pay the remaining MRC for the entire unexpired lock-in duration plus unrecovered capital costs.
All financial thresholds mentioned in the document are strictly in Indian Rupees (INR).
The proposed margin must meet the VIL baseline Internal Rate of Return (IRR) hurdle of 15%.
The total cost of ownership (Capex + Opex) must be modeled across the standard 3-year or 5-year contract term.
The Head of Enterprise Commercials must sign off on commercial and financial approvals.
Deal Desk escalation is triggered if projected margin or IRR is below 15%.
Deal Desk escalation is triggered if liability caps exceed 100% of the 12-month trailing contract value.
Deal Desk escalation is triggered for Capital Expenditure (Capex) requirements exceeding INR 5 Crores upfront.
The Commercial Packaging Guide provides a standardized framework for structuring, pricing, and bundling managed enterprise services.
All managed enterprise services must ensure a minimum 15% Internal Rate of Return (IRR) for margin protection.
All pricing metrics and thresholds are defined exclusively in Indian Rupees (INR), excluding applicable Goods and Services Tax (GST) unless explicitly noted.
The baseline currency for contract generation is Indian Rupees (INR).
Multi-currency contracts are only permitted via Deal Desk escalation for global subsidiaries operating within India.
Enterprise IoT/Mobility Aggregation is priced on a shared data pool (cAPN) model rather than per-SIM linear pricing.
Advanced Threat Protection (ATP) adds a +15% uplift on the CPE MRC per site.
entries.source_section: Service Add-On | Pricing Logic | Deployment Condition

entries.text: Dedicated SOC Analyst is priced at INR 15 Lakhs ARC (Annualized / Per FTE).
entries.source_section: Service Add-On | Pricing Logic | Deployment Condition

entries.text: Baseline connectivity must be quoted with optional managed add-ons to increase ARPU and client stickiness.
Managed 4G/5G Wireless Backup is priced as a flat MRC per site (Includes SIM + Data Pool).
Advanced Threat Protection (ATP) requires a +15% uplift on the CPE MRC per site.
Dedicated SOC Analyst pricing is INR 15 Lakhs ARC (Annualized / Per FTE).
Pricing discounts and Sales Incentive Plan payouts are heavily biased toward multi-year commitments.
12-month contract duration has a 0% discount (List Price) with AM Authority approval.
24-month contract duration allows up to 5% discount (Off List MRC) with AM Authority approval.
36-month contract duration allows up to 15% discount (Off List MRC) with Regional Sales Head approval.
If a customer refuses to pay the hardware OTC upfront, VI Business will finance the CPE.
The OTC is amortized across the contract term with a mandatory 12% annualized cost-of-capital markup added directly into the MRC line item.
Integrated 'Connectivity + Mobility + Cloud' constructs are strongly prioritized in Commercial Finance.
If a single MSA or RFP BoM contains SD-WAN, Enterprise IoT, and an Azure/AWS Interconnect, an additional 5% strategic discount on TCV is automatically applied.
Account Managers cannot waive initial setup fees (OTC) for custom fiber builds exceeding INR 50,000 without shifting the cost into a guaranteed 36-month minimum term MRC.
Customers may upgrade bandwidth dynamically via Bandwidth on Demand mid-contract but cannot downgrade below the original signed baseline commitment during the initial term.
RFPs must use Section 3 and Section 4 to answer questions about 'Service Tiering' and 'Value-Added Services'.
Pricing submitted in external RFPs must be consolidated and not expose the raw 12% cost-of-capital amortization formula to the customer.
Gold customers are defined as those with revenue exceeding INR 1 Crore.
Silver customers are defined as those with revenue below INR 1 Crore.
Quality Assurance must emphasize Section 4 (Experience Bands) to justify premium delivery vs. low-cost integrators.
National infrastructure value exceeds INR 2.0 Lakh Crore, representing the capitalized physical asset base (Optical, Core Routing, Radio).
Enterprise customer base exceeds 10,000 active clients, representing exclusively business (B2B) billing accounts.
Enterprise IoT connections exceed 2.5 million active endpoints, demonstrating uncontested dominance in Indian telematics billing.
Strategic direction pivots away from commoditized 'dumb pipe' connectivity toward high-margin, sticky Managed Digital Services.
The Enterprise (B2B) division represents the fastest-growing profitability segment within VIL.

Strategic investments have shifted from pure broadband to high-growth adjacency markets including SD-WAN & SASE Orchestration, Managed Security Operations (SOC), and Enterprise Mobility Management (Custom APNs).
Maintains a commanding enterprise revenue market share in India, securing anchor accounts within Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance (BFSI), IT/ITES, and Logistics verticals.
VI Business absorbs initial hardware capital expenditures exceeding INR 5 Crores on behalf of clients for complex, multi-site RFPs.
CapEx absorption is amortized via monthly recurring charges (MRC) across 36- or 60-month Master Services Agreements (MSAs).
Emphasize CapEx Absorption in Section 5 as a value-add during contract negotiations to transfer depreciation burden to VI Business.
The templates apply to all managed network, IoT, and SOC onboarding projects exceeding INR 1 Crore Total Contract Value (TCV).
Advance OTC invoice must be generated for billing.
Payment confirmation is required after generating an advance OTC invoice.
A formal commercial Change Request (CR) must be triggered if the customer alters the signed Bill of Materials (BoM).
Change Requests (CRs) must include the CR Reference, Initiator, Original Scope Constraint, Proposed Change, and Financial Impact (OTC/MRC).
The Base Fortinet 40F router is provided as the standard configuration.
Upgrading to Fortinet 80F for Branch 12 requires a +INR 15,000 one-time charge (OTC).
entries.source_section: Upgrade to Fortinet 80F for Branch 12 due to increased layout. +INR 15,000 OTC

entries.text: RFP Matrix Responses must include the exact structure of Section 3.1 (RAID Log) to demonstrate administrative maturity.
Scope-creep adjustments must be defended using Section 3.5 to justify formal commercial changes and protect internal margins.
All insurance policy limits are quoted in Indian Rupees (INR) or US Dollars (USD) where global reinsurance treaties apply, converted at the prevailing RBI reference rate.
Commercial General Liability has an aggregate annual limit of INR 250 Crores and per occurrence limit of INR 50 Crores.
Professional Indemnity (E&O) has an aggregate annual limit of USD 50 Million and per occurrence limit of USD 10 Million.
Cyber Liability has an aggregate annual limit of USD 25 Million and per occurrence limit of USD 5 Million.
The USD 50 Million E&O policy does not imply VI Business accepts a USD 50 Million liability cap in customer contracts.
VI Business's total liability is hard-capped at 100% of the trailing 12-month Total Contract Value (TCV) in standard commercial MSAs.
VI Business will not build custom middleware translation scripts on behalf of the customer unless contracted under a separate Professional Services BoM.
Penalty-free termination of contracts is prohibited due to the risk of destroying deal profitability from absorbed CapEx.
Adjusting Loss of Life (LoL) > 100% Total Contract Value (TCV) requires Enterprise CFO (Deal Desk) approval within 72 hours.
SIP Trunking Call Detail Records (CDR) must be generated dynamically and accessible via the Self-Care portal.
Consolidated Invoicing must roll up multiple regional branches into a single HQ invoice, segregated by cost center or billing account number.
Managed SD-WAN architectures require a minimum Total Contract Value (TCV) > INR 25 Lakhs.
Smaller deployments default to standard unmanaged connectivity if TCV is below INR 25 Lakhs.
Net-new enterprise logos requesting hardware CapEx exceeding INR 1 Crore require a D&B credit score review.
The Proposal Pricing Assumptions Guide establishes a strict framework of caveats and preconditions for all commercial quotes issued by VI Business (India).
Pricing assumptions expire 90 days from the original proposal submission date.
If an RFP extends beyond 90 days, commercial tables must be re-validated by the Deal Desk against prevailing OEM hardware exchange rates.
The scope of this document covers Managed SD-WAN, Enterprise SIP Trunking, Colocation, and IoT MSAs.
Every commercial proposal must include the 'Standard Commercial Annexure,' with pricing derived mathematically from defined assumptions.
entries.source_section: 3. The Assumption Framework and Legal Binding

entries.text: VI Business will not bear the cost of local municipal extortion or 'society fees'.
A 'Fruitless Visit' surcharge of INR 5,000 per engineer, per day applies if a field engineer is dispatched but the site is locked or denied entry by customer security.
Wall-mounting brackets or bespoke ruggedized enclosures must be explicitly declared and costed in the BoM.
Quoted solution terminates cleanly at the router's LAN port; active reconfiguration of customer's downstream LAN switching architecture is strictly charged as Professional Services.
All quotes, purchase orders, invoices, and payments must be exclusively executed in Indian Rupees (INR).
All stated prices are strictly exclusive of Goods and Services Tax (GST) and applicable statutory cesses/levies.
GST at the prevailing DoT/Finance Ministry rate (18% for telecom services) will be applied at the time of final invoicing.
INR OTC remains valid only if USD-to-INR exchange rate fluctuates by no more than +/- 5% during the 90-day proposal validity window for USD-denominated hardware imports.
Deduplication/optimization tuning (e.g., rewriting ERP TCP sliding windows) is not included in Day-2 operations.
Writing Python/Java scripts to integrate standard Webhooks/APIs into customer's legacy SAP/ServiceNow instance is outside pricing scope.
Providing legal or cryptocurrency negotiation services with ransomware threat actors is entirely out of scope.
Commercial/Financial risks include currency fluctuations impacting OEM hardware imports and massive SLA penalty triggers.
The manual is designed to secure high-availability contracts in the BFSI, ITES, and continuous-manufacturing sectors.
The internal Billing Operations team must automatically initiate Service Credit calculations for SLA breaches without requiring customer claims.
entries.source_section: 7. Root Cause Management and Recovery Governance

entries.text: The document type is classified as 'pricing' and the source filename is 'Service Description Documents.pdf'.
Bespoke, multi-page custom reporting beyond the standard Monthly SLA PDF is excluded and requires Professional Services SoW.
entries.source_section: Explicit Exclusions (Not covered, requires Professional Services SoW)

entries.text: Writing entirely custom Python/API middleware to integrate the VI Orchestrator into the customer's legacy ERP platform is excluded and requires Professional Services SoW.
entries.source_section: Explicit Exclusions (Not covered, requires Professional Services SoW)

entries.text: Billing commences precisely on the 'Service Commencement Date' (SCD), declared when the Field Engineer completes the SAT and proves the router can ping the central Orchestrator.
If a site is mathematically ready for billing but the customer delays final migration due to internal IT team unavailability, 'Deemed Acceptance' triggers billing regardless of the delay.
Scope applies to commercial proposals exceeding INR 25 Lakhs TCV (Managed Networks, Security, IoT, Voice).
Integration with legacy SAP instances requires a Professional Services Statement of Work (SoW) scoping, excluded from this baseline quote.
The commercial proposal is valid for exactly ninety (90) days from the submission date of [Date].
Pricing is contingent upon standard fiber feasibility. Extraordinary Right of Way (RoW) trenching charges will be passed through to [Customer Name] at cost.
All quoted prices (OTC and MRC) are exclusive of the prevailing 18% Goods and Services Tax.
Sales & Pre-Sales must use this document as a factual artifact for RFP responses questioning integration capabilities.
Custom rate limit elevations (+500 TPS) are available exclusively under dedicated Commercial Priority Support contracts.
On-Site Instructor-Led (Premium) training is reserved for TCV deals exceeding INR 10 Crore.
